title: Logging in as another user as an admin
excerpt: In other platforms I have been able to login as someone else to see what they are seeing. This is very helpful when I would give a new user access and want to see what they see so I know there are no issues when onboardi…

posts.author: Jtattan (Jillian Tattan )
posts.cookedHtml: In other platforms I have been able to login as someone else to see what they are seeing. This is very helpful when I would give a new user access and want to see what they see so I know there are no issues when onboarding. I know that there would most likely be a need for permission to be granted by the user if existing due to private boards and such but this has always been a very helpful feature in my previous jobs.

posts.author: kanerogerson (Kane)
posts.cookedHtml:
It appears that this was introduced which on one hand is great, however on the other there is a one size fits all and every admin has access to every single member.

There are different teams working within our organisation and I would like that the admin only has this access for their relelvant team. Otherwise admin user credentials grant access to every single piece of information regardless of confidentiality or sensitivity.

For clarity I would like it introduced so that the admin who oversees team A should not have access to log in as someone from team B or team C and view any private board and all it’s content

posts.author: lAMhUAN21 (hUAN)
posts.cookedHtml: I love this idea. Many platforms allow “impersonation” for admin troubleshooting, and it speeds up onboarding immensely. Even if it requires user consent for private boards, it could save a lot of time and prevent access issues.
